name: rope_d128_rd64
description: Rotary Position Embedding (RoPE) with partial rotary dimension. head_dim=128, rotary_dim=64. Only the first 64 dimensions are rotated; the remaining 64 pass through unchanged. Captured from MiniMax M2.

num_tokens.description: Total number of tokens across all sequences.

num_qo_heads.description: Number of query heads (varies by model and TP config).

num_kv_heads.description: Number of KV heads (varies by model and TP config).

rotary_dim.description: Partial rotary dimension. Only the first 64 of 128 head dimensions are rotated.

max_position.description: Maximum position index for the cos/sin cache.

cos_sin_cache.description: Precomputed cos/sin cache. First half is cos, second half is sin.

q_out.description: Query tensor with rotary embedding applied.

k_out.description: Key tensor with rotary embedding applied.

reference:
import torch


@torch.no_grad()
def run(q, k, cos_sin_cache, positions, rope_theta):
    head_dim = 128
    rotary_dim = 64

    num_tokens = q.shape[0]
    positions = positions.flatten().to(torch.long)

    # Look up cos/sin from precomputed cache
    cos_sin = cos_sin_cache.index_select(0, positions)  # [num_tokens, rotary_dim]
    cos, sin = cos_sin.chunk(2, dim=-1)  # each [num_tokens, rotary_dim/2]

    def apply_rotary_emb(x, cos, sin):
        cos = cos.unsqueeze(-2).to(x.dtype)  # [num_tokens, 1, rotary_dim/2]
        sin = sin.unsqueeze(-2).to(x.dtype)  # [num_tokens, 1, rotary_dim/2]
        x1, x2 = torch.chunk(x, 2, dim=-1)  # each [num_tokens, num_heads, rotary_dim/2]
        o1 = x1 * cos - x2 * sin
        o2 = x2 * cos + x1 * sin
        return torch.cat((o1, o2), dim=-1)

    # Process Q: split rotary and pass-through parts
    q_f32 = q.to(torch.float32)
    q_shape = q_f32.shape
    q_3d = q_f32.view(num_tokens, -1, head_dim)
    q_rot = q_3d[..., :rotary_dim]
    q_pass = q_3d[..., rotary_dim:]
    q_rot = apply_rotary_emb(q_rot, cos, sin)
    q_out = torch.cat((q_rot, q_pass), dim=-1).reshape(q_shape).to(q.dtype)

    # Process K: split rotary and pass-through parts
    k_f32 = k.to(torch.float32)
    k_shape = k_f32.shape
    k_3d = k_f32.view(num_tokens, -1, head_dim)
    k_rot = k_3d[..., :rotary_dim]
    k_pass = k_3d[..., rotary_dim:]
    k_rot = apply_rotary_emb(k_rot, cos, sin)
    k_out = torch.cat((k_rot, k_pass), dim=-1).reshape(k_shape).to(k.dtype)

    return q_out, k_out
